rationale: The cited paper is used for a specific analytical role: the authors code temporal distance in their meta-analysis using a log distance-from-present factor and cite Zauberman et al. (2009) as the basis for that transformation. This is more than a generic background mention because it informs how a key moderator (time horizon) is operationalized in their tests. At the same time, the citation is not a central theoretical backbone of the article's main expense-neglect theory, but a targeted borrowing of a time-perception idea.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Zauberman et al. (2009) as precedent for treating objective time distance nonlinearly (log-transformed) when modeling horizon effects. It uses that subjective-time insight to test whether expense neglect increases for more distant future forecasts.
